1. The vlogger wants to create a schedule that maximizes the number of unique sequences of visiting these sites. The number of different sequences is calculated by the factorial of the number of sites, which is 7! = 5040. This ensures that each site is visited exactly once in a unique order.  
2. The vlogger also plans to spend a specific amount of time at each site. The time spent at each site follows a normal distribution with a mean of 3 hours and a standard deviation of 0.5 hours. The probability that the total time spent visiting all 7 sites will exceed 22 hours can be calculated using the normal distribution. The mean total time is 7 * 3 = 21 hours, and the standard deviation is sqrt(7) * 0.5 ≈ 1.3229. The z-score for 22 hours is (22 - 21) / 1.3229 ≈ 0.756. The probability that the total time exceeds 22 hours is the area under the normal curve to the right of z = 0.756, which is approximately 0.2257.
